symptoms.symptom_name: Painless full-thickness plantar ulcer under the first metatarsal head

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A painless plantar ulcer at a pressure point is the hallmark of a neuropathic diabetic foot ulcer.
Ischaemic ulcers are characteristically painful; painlessness points away from arterial aetiology.
Purulent malodorous discharge indicates established wound infection rather than simple colonisation.
Chronic malodorous discharge from a deep ulcer raises the probability of underlying bone infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Perilesional erythema extending approximately 1 cm from the wound edge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Perilesional erythema is the earliest sign of cellulitis; degree and spread determine urgency.
Surrounding erythema confirms the ulcer is infected rather than merely colonised.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, rigors or confusion (no systemic sepsis features)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever, rigors or confusion currently argues against systemic sepsis, but diabetic sepsis can be blunted — negative screen does not remove the need for safety-netting.
Establishing the absence of systemic features is required to keep the patient below the emergency-ambulance tier at this moment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Long-standing poorly controlled type 2 diabetes with poor adherence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperglycaemia impairs immunity and healing, raising both ulceration and infection probability.
Poor glycaemic control increases the risk of deep and bone infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bounding pedal pulses with warm, dry foot

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable bounding pulses and warm skin indicate preserved perfusion, excluding an ischaemic ulcer — a sealed clinician examination finding, not obtainable via chat.
Warm, dry (autonomic-neuropathy) skin with good pulses is consistent with a neuropathic rather than ischaemic ulcer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Loss of protective sensation to 10 g monofilament (bilateral)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objectively confirmed loss of protective sensation defines the neuropathic mechanism — a sealed examination finding; the AI infers neuropathy from the painless incidental history.

investigation_details.investigation_name: Probe-to-bone test and plain X-ray of the foot
investigation_details.expected_result: Deep wound; if probe contacts bone or X-ray shows cortical erosion/periosteal reaction this supports osteomyelitis — cannot be established remotely
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Osteomyelitis remains undiagnosed; antibiotic choice and duration and the need for surgery are not correctly determined.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive probe-to-bone test substantially raises the probability of osteomyelitis in a diabetic foot ulcer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Charcot foot deformity with thick hyperkeratotic callus at the pressure point

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deformity concentrates plantar pressure and callus formation, the mechanical driver of neuropathic ulceration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Continued barefoot weight-bearing on the insensate foot

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ongoing unprotected loading perpetuates trauma and prevents healing — the target of offloading management.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A painless plantar ulcer at a pressure point + purulent discharge + loss of protective sensation + deformity/callus is the defining neuropathic-ulcer constellation; the sum is a limb-threatening infected ulcer even though each feature alone seems unalarming, and the painlessness is a danger sign not a reassurance.
A deep, malodorous, chronically discharging ulcer that may probe to bone mandates active exclusion of osteomyelitis with in-person probing and imaging.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line bedside test for osteomyelitis in a deep diabetic foot ulcer — requires in-person assessment, a key reason to escalate.
Assess for osteomyelitis, gas, foreign body and Charcot changes; needed today given depth and infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab / deep tissue culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Guides targeted antibiotic therapy for the infected ulcer; taken at in-person assessment ideally after debridement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: FBC, CRP, HbA1c and renal function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess systemic infection/inflammation, glycaemic control and renal function for antibiotic dosing; cannot be obtained via telehealth.
